claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

First, let me check the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child, Alex, who's 3 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 3 years old and lives with you.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask a few more questions:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. You live in the UK in Manchester and have Alex (age 3) living with you.

Next question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

One more important question: **Are you or Alex's other parent (or partner, if you have one) earning over £60,000 per year individually?**

(This won't affect whether you're eligible to receive Child Benefit, but it may mean you have to pay a tax charge, so it's useful to know.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing all that information.

Just to confirm a few final details: **Is Alex in any of these situations:**
- In hospital or residential accommodation for an extended period?
- In local authority care?
- Or are there any other special circumstances about Alex's living situation I should know about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply. Alex is just living at home with me, nothing unusual about the situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided, let me evaluate your situation:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 3 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex's care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- There are no special circumstances affecting eligibility

You meet all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Additionally, since neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you won't have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

You can proceed with applying for Child Benefit for Alex. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK (Manchester), and you are responsible for their care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 3-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex who lives with them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no special circumstances (such as the child being in care or hospital). The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Neither parent earns over £60,000, so there would be no High Income Child Benefit tax charge to pay.

Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 3-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex who lives with them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no special circumstances (such as the child being in care or hospital). The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Neither parent earns over £60,000, so there would be no High Income Child Benefit tax charge to pay.
